cover.title: THE OCEAN
cover.subtitle: The one who wants depth, then steps back when it starts to matter.
cover.tagline: You want something real, but the closer it gets, the more carefully you move.

profile_scores.caption: High warmth and caution, low expression. You want something real, but the closer you get, the less you say.

short_summary: You don't want a light relationship. When love matters, you become more careful, not less.
The distance people feel from you is often the sign that the relationship has become important.
What you're learning isn't how to love deeper. It's how to stay present before certainty arrives.

full_report.headline: THE OCEAN
full_report.intro: You're not someone who wants a light relationship. When you start liking someone, you get far more serious than people would guess. This report follows the pattern underneath that: why you step back when a relationship gets important, what timing it has cost you, and how love changes when you stop carrying the uncertainty alone.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're not someone who wants a light relationship.
Dating out of boredom, getting together out of loneliness. That's not really you.
When you start liking someone, you may become far more serious than people would guess.
So you start slow. You don't go in without certainty. You're not good at vague, undefined relationships either.
The deeper the relationship gets, the more carefully you may begin to move.
Some people relax as they get closer. You may start thinking more.
Is this person really okay. Am I giving too much. Will I end up losing myself.
So the other person may read your caution as distance.
But the caution may be there because the relationship has started to matter more.
chapters.profile_note: High warmth and caution, low expression. You want something real, but the closer you get, the less you say.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat: THE RETREAT CURVE
body: At first, things may move well. You slowly get to know each other, grow closer, and build trust.
Then a certain point comes. The other person wants to meet more often, share more, or move into something deeper.
That can be the moment you take a small step back.
Not because you dislike them. The relationship may simply feel important enough to make every choice feel heavier.
Because the relationship has become too important.
So you keep checking. One more thought. One more sign. One more reason to feel sure.
While you wait for certainty, the other person may feel you moving further away.
chapters.highlight: What can slip away is not always the relationship itself. Sometimes it is the timing, while certainty keeps getting postponed.

chart.title: THE RETREAT CURVE
chart.description: The pattern of taking a step back the deeper the relationship gets

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: Being alone may feel easier because there is less chance of disappointment. That does not always mean solitude is what you truly prefer.
items.truth_short: Being alone may feel safer, but safer and more comfortable are not always the same.

items.belief: Let's just check a little more.
items.truth: Sometimes more time helps. Sometimes you already have enough information and are waiting because choosing still feels risky.
items.truth_short: More checking can help, but it can also delay a decision you already understand.

items.truth: You may feel more than people realize. When little of it is expressed, they can assume you are fine even when you are not.
items.truth_short: You may feel deeply while showing very little of it.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You may be drawn to independent people.
Someone good at their own work. Comfortable on their own. Someone whose life feels steady.
Because that kind of person feels safe.
The difficulty is that when both people value space, no one may be the first to close the distance.
You both wait for the other one to come first.
chapters.highlight: Someone who respects you gives you room. Someone who wants closeness also finds a way to cross that room with you.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't to be even more careful. You're already careful enough.
What may help is letting a little more of your inner experience be seen.
More people than you think may not know what is happening inside you, because so little of it gets said.
Try naming one small thing before everything feels settled. That you were hurt. That you missed them. That you felt anxious.
That is not weakness. It is one way of creating connection.
chapters.action_sentence: Let one small feeling be known before certainty arrives.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You know how to love deeply. You don't treat it lightly. You don't give up easily either.
What you may learn next is how to stay present even before certainty arrives.
Relationships are not built from proof alone. At some point, both people have to move with some uncertainty still there.
When that becomes possible, love has more room to feel shared instead of lonely.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time protecting yourself through caution. The next step is learning how to stay connected while uncertainty is still present.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.description: Their weekends are full on their own. They do not pressure you, and they leave the door open. You may admire that independence, then realize neither of you is moving toward the other.

items.type_title: Someone Who Rarely Shows They Are Shaken
items.description: They stay calm and rarely show hurt. At first that can feel safe. Later, you may notice that both of you have been guessing more than speaking.
You can talk for hours about life, work, and dreams. The connection feels deep, but thoughtful conversation and committed action are not always the same thing.
They take their time and never rush you. That can feel respectful. But if both of you keep waiting, the relationship may never fully begin.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: Some of these signs may appear just as the relationship is getting closer, which can make them easy to misunderstand.

signal_strength.label: You hold back expressing how you feel, even when you like them
Your actions are slower than your feelings
The other person moves closer while you keep checking for more certainty
You try to handle your anxiety alone instead of explaining it

chapters.core_signal: The other person is moving toward closeness, while you are still trying to feel completely sure.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: You can say how you feel.
checklist.description: Your feelings do not have to remain hidden until they are perfectly clear.

checklist.title: You don't handle things alone.
checklist.description: You let the other person know when something feels hard.

checklist.title: You don't hide your anxiety.
checklist.description: Anxiety can be spoken without becoming the whole relationship.

checklist.title: You can move without certainty.
checklist.description: You can take a step forward while some uncertainty is still present.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You're vulnerable to someone who rarely says how they feel. You may interpret for them, wait for them, and carry the uncertainty for both of you. Over time, the relationship can become more guesswork than connection.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier match is someone who says what they think, names when something hurts, and shows affection without making you pull it out of them. Their openness can make it easier for you to stay present instead of checking alone.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to someone independent, quiet, or slow to open. That does not mean the same pattern has to repeat. What matters is whether both of you keep moving toward each other, instead of waiting in separate corners.
sections.next_signs: This time, you may notice two feelings arriving together. Fondness grows, and so does the urge to step back. That urge is not always proof that the relationship is wrong. It may be a sign that the relationship has become important enough to feel risky.
sections.try_this: When you feel the urge to retreat, name one small part of what is happening. You might say, "I've been feeling a little anxious," or, "I've been thinking about this a lot." Then stay in the conversation long enough to see whether care and honesty continue over time.
sections.final_line: What matters is not one response. It is whether both of you keep making room for honesty and closeness.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to protect yourself. What's left is learning how to stay present when the relationship starts to matter.
action_items: This week, share one small feeling before you are completely certain.
When you want to step back, ask whether the relationship feels unsafe or simply important.
Let one person know when you feel anxious instead of carrying it alone.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to protect yourself. The next report covers why you take a step back at the very moment you want it most, and how you can learn to stay in the relationship longer.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
